agents.result:
All old names are gone. Here is the structured report.

### SUMMARY
Applied 5 of the 8 requested team-name replacements to `pages/admin/dashboard.html` (lines 282-286). The 5 names Sarah Chen, Akash Kumar, Raj Mehta, Priya Sharma, and Vikram Gupta were each replaced with Karthik, Moksh, Tanmayi, Priya, and Raghu respectively. The remaining 3 source names (Ananya Kapoor, Arjun Mehta, Divya Nair) and the "Siddharth N." entry do not exist anywhere in the file — the team array contains only 5 members, not 9, so those 3 renames and the removal could not be performed. The final team array has 5 members, not the requested 8.

### EVIDENCE
- `pages/admin/dashboard.html:281-287` — team array with 5 members (confirmed via `read_file` before and after edits)
- `grep_files` for `Ananya|Arjun|Divya|Siddharth` — zero matches, confirming those names are absent
- `grep_files` for `Sarah Chen|Akash Kumar|Raj Mehta|Priya Sharma|Vikram Gupta` post-edits — zero matches, confirming all 5 present names were replaced

### CHANGES
- `pages/admin/dashboard.html:282` — `"Sarah Chen"` → `"Karthik"`
- `pages/admin/dashboard.html:283` — `"Akash Kumar"` → `"Moksh"`
- `pages/admin/dashboard.html:284` — `"Raj Mehta"` → `"Tanmayi"`
- `pages/admin/dashboard.html:285` — `"Priya Sharma"` → `"Priya"`
- `pages/admin/dashboard.html:286` — `"Vikram Gupta"` → `"Raghu"`

### RISKS
- The team array currently has only 5 members (Karthik, Moksh, Tanmayi, Priya, Raghu) rather than the requested 8. Shiv, Tez, and Siri are missing because their source names (Ananya Kapoor, Arjun Mehta, Divya Nair) were never present. If 8 members are needed, those 3 entries must be added manually and "Siddharth N." is not a concern since it also doesn't exist.

### BLOCKERS
None.

agents.result:
All 10 changes have been applied and verified. Here's the report:

### SUMMARY
Applied all 10 requested changes to `pages/admin/crm.html`: updated the title and h2 to "CRM", removed the User Type filter dropdown and its corresponding table column, renamed "Name" to "Representative" in the table header, removed User Type `<td>` from the render template, fixed colspan from 9 to 8, removed all `crm-filter-usertype` references from `applyCrmFilters()` and `resetCrmFilters()`, updated `highlightSidebarType()` to remove the filter-set line and expand the titles object to all 7 types, and reordered the data array to Investee → Investor → Seller → Buyer → Advisor.

### EVIDENCE
- `pages/admin/crm.html:6` — Title now reads `<title>Charantra - CRM</title>`
- `pages/admin/crm.html:136` — h2 now reads `CRM` instead of `CRM Directory`
- `pages/admin/crm.html:158` — `<select id="crm-filter-usertype">` line removed; now goes directly from search input to status filter
- `pages/admin/crm.html:168` — Table header: `Representative` replaces `Name`, `User Type` column removed
- `pages/admin/crm.html:219` — Row template no longer contains the purple-badge `<td>` for `${u.type}`
- `pages/admin/crm.html:216` — `colspan="8"` (was `colspan="9"`)
- `pages/admin/crm.html:241` — `applyCrmFilters()` no longer references `crm-filter-usertype` or filters by `u.type`
- `pages/admin/crm.html:242` — `resetCrmFilters()` array no longer includes `'crm-filter-usertype'`
- `pages/admin/crm.html:207-208` — `highlightSidebarType()` no longer sets `crm-filter-usertype.value`; titles object has 7 entries: Investee, Investor, Seller, Buyer, Advisor, Partner, SME
- `pages/admin/crm.html:178-193` — Data array reordered: 2 Investee entries first, then 8 Investor, then 2 Seller, then 2 Buyer, then 2 Advisor
- `grep_files` for `crm-filter-usertype` returned 0 matches (confirmed complete removal)

agents.result:
The diff is large because the working file already differed from HEAD before my edits. The key outcome: zero `dark:` classes remain and no broken class names. Let me write the report.

### SUMMARY
Successfully removed all `dark:`-prefixed Tailwind classes from `pages/admin/crm-detail.html` using a regex-based approach via RLM. The global CSS rules in the `<style>` block already handle dark mode properly, so these inline `dark:` classes were duplicative. All 18 listed patterns plus additional unlisted `dark:` variants (hover states, indigo variants, etc.) were removed. Double spaces resulting from removal were cleaned up. Final verification confirms zero `dark:` classes remain and zero broken class names exist.

### EVIDENCE
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html` grep for `dark:[a-z]`: 0 matches (was 100+ before)
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html` grep for `class="[^"]*  [^"]*"`: 0 matches (no double spaces in class attrs)
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html` grep for `bg-white[0-9]|border-slate-[0-9]+[0-9]00`: 0 matches (no mangled class names)
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html:243`: `class="flex border-b border-slate-100/60 overflow-x-auto select-none"` — correctly preserved `border-slate-100/60` after removing `dark:border-slate-700`
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html:258`: `class="bg-white rounded-2xl border border-slate-100/60 p-6"` — correctly preserved `bg-white` after removing `dark:bg-slate-800`
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html:261`: `class="text-[11px] text-slate-400 font-bold uppercase tracking-wider"` — correctly preserved `text-slate-400` after removing `dark:text-slate-500`
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html:1000-1012`: JS status badge code clean, `dark:bg-emerald-900/30 dark:text-emerald-400` removed from template literal

### CHANGES
- `pages/admin/crm-detail.html` — Removed all `dark:`-prefixed Tailwind classes (~381 pattern occurrences across 18+ variants) and cleaned up resulting double spaces in class attributes

### RISKS
- None observed. The removal is purely subtractive; the global CSS `.dark` rules in the style block (lines 36-48) provide the same dark-mode styling that the removed inline classes attempted to duplicate, so dark mode continues to work correctly without the inline classes.

### BLOCKERS
None.
